expectations.text: Recommends `winml inspect` before any build/benchmark work.
expectations.evidence: No winml inspect. Goes straight to `optimum-cli export onnx`.

expectations.text: Identifies QNN as the EP for Snapdragon X Elite NPU.
expectations.evidence: 'NPU (QNN EP): the Hexagon NPU on X Elite effectively requires quantized models'.

expectations.text: Recommends building once and running `winml perf` twice.
expectations.evidence: Exports once but then quantizes specifically for NPU (creating a separate QDQ file), then benchmarks. Doesn't have the 'shared build, two perf runs over different EP artifacts' framing â€” runs CPU on FP32 and NPU on INT8 with different files.

expectations.text: Specifies that CPU run uses optimized (pre-compile) artifact, NOT QNN-compiled.
expectations.evidence: Has no notion of a 'compiled artifact' since it doesn't use the winml pipeline. CPU uses FP32 ONNX, NPU uses separately-quantized QDQ file.
Never mentions EP-locking. Some QNN gotchas (silent CPU fallback, ARM64 build) but not this specific failure mode.

expectations.text: Directs to --help or uses common patterns; no fabricated flags.
All `winml` commands quoted in the response are runnable — every flag exists in `winml <cmd> --help`, no positional model arg where the CLI is flag-only.
expectations.evidence: Response uses no winml commands (trivially passes).
